description: masscan is a Python wrapper around masscan, an asynchronous TCP port scanner.

documentation.example: import masscan; m = masscan.PortScanner(); print(m.scan('10.0.0.0/24', ports='22,80'))
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/masscan && python3 masscan.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/masscan && python3 masscan.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/masscan && python3 masscan.py --help`. masscan wrapper (high-speed port scanner).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: masscan wrapper (high-speed port scanner).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: masscan wrapper (high-speed port scanner).

metadata.example: import masscan; m = masscan.PortScanner(); print(m.scan('10.0.0.0/24', ports='22,80'))

name: masscan
